summary: Solid test suite with good bats structure covering main requirements (label assignment, multiple labels, deduplication, priority ordering, glob patterns, file argument mode). Tests are well-organized with clear names and proper use of setup(). However, coverage gaps exist: invalid config rules, special characters in paths/labels, config edge cases (only comments, malformed lines), priority tiebreaking, and stress testing are untested. Rigor is limited primarily to happy path; only basic error conditions (missing files) are tested, not invalid inputs or comprehensive error messages. Design is clean but fragile: tests require fixture files (basic.conf, priority.conf, comments.conf, changed-files.txt) and workflow file not shown, and external dependencies (Python3 yaml module, actionlint) could cause failures. Tests assume these fixtures exist but don't show their setup/creation. Would catch common regressions but lacks depth for edge cases and error scenarios.
